chapter_info.subject: Science
chapter_info.chapter_name: Adolescence: A Stage of Growth and Change

flashcards.q: Between which ages does adolescence generally occur?
flashcards.a: Between 10 and 19 years of age.

flashcards.q: What are the five stages of human life in order?
flashcards.a: Infancy → Childhood → Adolescence → Adulthood → Old age.

flashcards.q: At approximately what age do changes beyond height and weight begin to appear in humans?
flashcards.a: Around 10–12 years of age.

flashcards.q: True or False: Adolescence lasts for the same duration in every individual.
flashcards.a: False. Its duration varies from person to person.

flashcards.q: What is the voice box also called?
flashcards.a: Larynx.

flashcards.q: In which sex is the voice box growth more prominent during adolescence?
flashcards.a: Boys.

flashcards.q: Is the Adam's apple visible in every individual?
flashcards.a: No, it is not noticeable in every individual.

flashcards.q: Name the skin condition caused by clogged pores due to increased oily secretions during adolescence.
flashcards.a: Acne.

flashcards.q: Are pimples and acne the same thing?
flashcards.a: No. Acne is the condition; pimples are its visible manifestation.

flashcards.q: In which body regions does hair growth occur in both boys and girls during adolescence?
flashcards.a: Armpits and pubic region.

flashcards.q: What type of hair growth is specific to boys during adolescence?
flashcards.a: Facial hair — moustache and beard; and possibly chest and back hair.

flashcards.q: Name one secondary sexual characteristic specific to girls.
flashcards.a: Development of breasts.

flashcards.q: Do secondary sexual characteristics directly involve reproduction?
flashcards.a: No. They distinguish males from females but are not directly involved in reproduction.

flashcards.q: What marks the onset of puberty?
flashcards.a: The appearance of secondary sexual characteristics.

flashcards.q: What is the typical range of the menstrual cycle duration in healthy girls?
flashcards.a: 21 to 35 days (most commonly 28–30 days).

flashcards.q: How long does menstruation (blood discharge phase) typically last?
flashcards.a: 3 to 7 days.

flashcards.q: At approximately what age does menstruation naturally stop in women?
flashcards.a: Between 45 and 55 years of age.

flashcards.q: Is menstruation a sign of illness?
flashcards.a: No. It is a natural, healthy sign of good reproductive health.

flashcards.q: Do myths about menstruation have any scientific basis?
flashcards.a: No. All myths and taboos about menstruation have no scientific basis.

flashcards.q: Name the government helpline number for drug addiction and de-addiction in India.
flashcards.a: 14446 (National De-addiction Helpline).

flashcards.q: What is the full form of RKSK?
flashcards.a: Rashtriya Kishor Swasthya Karyakram.

flashcards.q: Which initiative provides biodegradable sanitary pads through Jan Aushadhi Kendras?
flashcards.a: Suvidha Sanitary Napkin Initiative.

flashcards.q: Which government scheme provides free or subsidised sanitary pads to adolescent girls in rural areas?
flashcards.a: Menstrual Hygiene Scheme (MHS).

flashcards.q: Name the state-level scheme in Karnataka that promotes menstrual hygiene.
flashcards.a: Shuchi Scheme.

flashcards.q: Which campaign was launched by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment to prevent substance abuse?
flashcards.a: Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan.

flashcards.q: Name two minerals that are essential for bone growth during adolescence.
flashcards.a: Calcium and phosphorus.

flashcards.q: Which nutrient deficiency can cause anaemia, especially in adolescent girls?
flashcards.a: Iron (or Vitamin B12) deficiency.

flashcards.q: Who was Dorothy Hodgkin and what is her connection to adolescent health?
flashcards.a: She was a Nobel Prize-winning chemist (1964) who studied the structure of Vitamin B12 — essential for proper body functioning.

flashcards.q: Name two food sources rich in iron.
flashcards.a: Spinach and kidney beans (also raisins, figs).

flashcards.q: Name two food sources rich in calcium.
flashcards.a: Milk and curd (also cheese, paneer, millets).

flashcards.q: What nutrients do pulses and meat primarily provide?
flashcards.a: Proteins.

flashcards.q: True or False: Hormones are produced only in one part of the body.
flashcards.a: False. Hormones are produced in different parts (glands) of the body.

flashcards.q: How do hormones reach their target organs?
flashcards.a: They are released into the bloodstream and travel to target organs.

flashcards.q: Do hormones influence mood and behaviour?
flashcards.a: Yes. Some hormones influence mood and behaviour.

flashcards.q: Name the primary cause of most physical changes during adolescence.
flashcards.a: Hormones — chemical messengers produced in the body in response to signals from the brain.

flashcards.q: What emotional change causes adolescents to feel very energetic on some days and low on others?
flashcards.a: Mood swings.

flashcards.q: Name one positive outcome of strong emotions during adolescence.
flashcards.a: Increased compassion and involvement in social work (or creativity and innovation).

flashcards.q: What is cyberbullying?
flashcards.a: Use of digital devices to harass others by sending misleading messages, spreading false rumours, or sharing personal information without consent.

flashcards.q: True or False: It is safe to share personal photos with virtual friends online.
flashcards.a: False. Personal photos should not be shared with strangers or virtual friends.

flashcards.q: Name any two harmful substances adolescents are advised to avoid.
flashcards.a: Tobacco and alcohol (also: beedi, gutka, cigarettes, illegal drugs).

flashcards.q: What makes harmful substances particularly dangerous — besides health damage?
flashcards.a: They are addictive — they create a persistent urge to use them again and again.

flashcards.q: What is the first step towards overcoming addiction?
flashcards.a: Seeking help and support from family, friends, parents, or teachers; counselling and medical advice.

flashcards.q: Name two benefits of regular physical activity during adolescence.
flashcards.a: Keeps body fit and healthy; builds stamina and boosts mood (any two valid answers).

flashcards.q: True or False: Variations in the timing of physical changes during adolescence indicate a health problem.
flashcards.a: False. All such variations are completely normal.

flashcards.q: What does it mean that reproductive changes during adolescence are 'internal'?
flashcards.a: They occur inside the body (e.g., maturation of reproductive organs) and are not visible from the outside.
